gaps: statpack relist-count and CVSG cuts are '(unknown)' for the entire modern-cert population, so the single strongest cert signal (serial relisting) has no quantitative anchor
fedcourts query has no free-text/topic filter that would surface doctrinally similar petitions (e.g. other Second Amendment cert petitions); citation filters returned nothing

a corpus view of same-conference sibling outcomes (what was granted/denied/held at conference X) would have made the June 30 hold-vs-deny signal directly queryable instead of inferred from a broad era query
helpful: provisioned docket snapshot current to 2026-07-10 (the full distribution/relist history was the decisive input)
provisioned questions-presented, petition, and brief-in-opposition text
fedcourts query --era 2020s (surfaced the 2026-06-30 grants in Viramontes/Grant and same-day denials of sibling 2A petitions)
web search (identified the Benson split and the limited QP of the Viramontes/Grant grant)
notes: Corpus priors were unexpectedly the highest-value retrieval: a plain era-filtered query happened to return the companion hardware-ban dockets with their 2026-06-30 cert-granted dates and distribution counts, which established that Duncan was held (not denied) at the end-of-term conference. Two ranged queries cost ~105 MB of GETs each, which feels heavy for a handful of rows.
